title: OpenClaw
summary: A practical starting runtime for learners who want a controllable local assistant environment.
maturityLabel: Most production-ready of the learning runtimes
recommendedFor: beginners who want the safest first runtime
learners building a local-first spine project
students who want clearer debugging loops
avoidIf: you need an ultra-lightweight experiment on constrained hardware
you want persistence-focused memory behavior before mastering the basics
supportNotes: Use this as the academy default unless the learner has a very specific runtime constraint.
cons: Can feel operationally heavy for absolute non-technical learners

title: Hermes Agent / Hermet
summary: A persistence-oriented runtime for learners exploring memory-aware assistants.
maturityLabel: Promising but requires more operational care
recommendedFor: learners ready to compare short-lived sessions with persistent behavior
avoidIf: this is your first runtime install
you still need a low-friction confidence-building setup
supportNotes: Use after OpenClaw foundations or when the curriculum specifically teaches persistence and memory validation.
cons: Not the safest default first runtime

title: NanoClaw
summary: A lighter runtime variant for constrained or more experimental local setups.
maturityLabel: Use for comparison and exploration, not the default beginner path
recommendedFor: learners curious about tradeoffs in constrained runtime design
avoidIf: you want the safest beginner path
you need stable support expectations for a capstone
supportNotes: Position this as a deliberate comparison exercise rather than the first runtime learners should bet on.
cons: Should not be the default path for new learners
